title: thermopsis lanceolata subsp. turkestanica
intro: Thermopsis lanceolata subsp. turkestanica, commonly known as Turkestanese or Yellow Mountain Lily, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic effects. The plant's extracts have been found to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. Thermopsis lanceolata subsp. turkestanica can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and has been used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
